_comment: Curated CVE corpus for the RAG grounding experiment. Each entry is a real NVD CVE with the affected PRODUCT and VERSION in the description so semantic retrieval matches on the fingerprinted software, not just the service word. Covers the four in-scope services (ftp/ssh/smb/http) with both on-target and realistic off-target entries, so retrieval quality — not a cherry-picked set — is what's being tested. service tags are normalized: ftp|ssh|smb|http.

cves.description: vsftpd 2.3.4 contains a backdoor: a username ending in ':)' opens a command shell on TCP 6200, allowing unauthenticated remote command execution.
ProFTPD 1.3.5 mod_copy SITE CPFR/CPTO commands allow unauthenticated remote attackers to copy files and achieve remote code execution.
Stack-based buffer overflow in the Telnet IAC handling of ProFTPD before 1.3.3c allows remote code execution.
Use-after-free in ProFTPD 1.3.6/1.3.7 via the SSH connection handler may allow remote code execution.
Arbitrary file copy in ProFTPD via mod_copy allows remote unauthenticated attackers to read or write files outside the FTP root.

Predictable random number generator in the Debian/Ubuntu OpenSSL package (2006-2008) produces weak OpenSSH host and user keys that can be brute-forced; affects OpenSSH on Debian-based systems including OpenSSH 4.x.
libssh 0.6 through 0.8.3 authentication bypass: a client can send SSH2_MSG_USERAUTH_SUCCESS to a libssh-based server to authenticate without credentials. Does not affect OpenSSH.
OpenSSH before 7.3 allows remote username enumeration via timing differences in password authentication of long passwords for invalid users.
OpenSSH through 7.7 allows remote username enumeration by responding differently to malformed authentication requests for valid vs invalid users.
Signal handler race condition in OpenSSH before 4.4 may allow remote code execution or denial of service; affects OpenSSH 4.x including 4.7.
Samba 3.0.0 through 3.0.25rc3 'username map script' command injection allows unauthenticated remote command execution via shell metacharacters in the username; affects Linux Samba smbd 3.x.
SambaCry: Samba 3.5.0 through 4.x allows a writable-share client to upload and load a shared library, achieving remote code execution on Linux Samba servers.
Uninitialized pointer in the Samba smbd netlogon server (3.5.x-4.x) allows unauthenticated remote code execution.
EternalBlue (MS17-010): a remote code execution flaw in Microsoft Windows SMBv1. Affects Windows hosts only; not Linux Samba.
SMBGhost: a wormable remote code execution flaw in Microsoft Windows 10 SMBv3 compression. Affects Windows SMBv3 only; not Linux Samba.
Shellshock: GNU Bash through 4.3 executes trailing commands in specially crafted environment variables, allowing remote code execution through Apache mod_cgi/CGI scripts that invoke bash.
Incomplete fix for Shellshock in GNU Bash still allows attackers to write files or run commands via crafted environment variables through CGI.
Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x Range header denial of service ('Apache Killer'): a crafted Range header causes excessive memory and CPU consumption. Affects Apache 2.2.8.
Apache httpd 2.2.x mod_proxy reverse proxy exposure: a crafted request URI allows access to internal servers behind the proxy.
Denial of service in mod_proxy of Apache httpd 2.0.x and 2.2.x via a request that triggers an infinite loop when a remote server is slow.
Path traversal and remote code execution in Apache httpd 2.4.49 via crafted '..%2e' sequences when mod_cgi is enabled. Affects 2.4.49 only, not 2.2.x.
PHP before 5.3.12/5.4.2 in CGI mode mishandles query strings lacking '=', allowing remote attackers to pass command-line arguments to php-cgi and execute code.
Apache httpd 2.2.x mod_isapi on Windows allows remote code execution by unloading an ISAPI module while a request is processing. Windows only.
